film.title: Dholak
film.overview: Roop K. Shorey’s romantic drama. Manohar (Ajit) gets a job as a music teacher in a college. But on one condition – he has to be married in order to do it. Also starring Meena Shorey. In Hindi/subs.

dialogues.dialogue: Through these letters which the postman gave me by mistake. And you must have read them as old habits die hard. Shameless. I want to give you good news. How many have called? - All of them I guess. Read.

I see. That's why you must have said that we're married. I knew that something's fishy. - It's no big deal. Don't you have any shame? You can either have shame or the job. I'll not stay here even for a minute. - Think about your parents.
dialogues.cause_raw: lying about marital status for job

dialogues.dialogue: I knew that something's fishy. - It's no big deal. Don't you have any shame? You can either have shame or the job. I'll not stay here even for a minute. - Think about your parents. What about the landlord's loan?
For the baby and the expectant mother. Oh. Shameless. Today I'll kill you. I haven't done anything. I swear. I'm innocent. You first made the excuse of marriage. And now this.
Till then good luck, Mr. and Mrs. Manohar. Now what? Disgrace. Shame. And jail. Our parents will be heartbroken. What do we do now, Manohar?

“Yesterday I saw a girl walking at Mall Road.” “Yesterday I saw a girl walking at Mall Road.” “She was walking proudly...”“She was walking proudly and her hanky fell.” “It picked it up for her and the girl smiled.” “Let's have a blast. Let's have a blast.”
